heading: Issuance of money orders without payment
label: 1713 Issuance of money orders without payment
text: Whoever, being an officer or employee of the Postal Service, issues a money order without having previously received the money therefor, shall be fined under this title.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 333 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 21035 Stat. 1129Minor change was made in phraseology.

Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $500”.Pub. L. 103–3221970— substituted “an officer or employee of the Postal Service” for “a postmaster or other person employed in any branch of the Postal Service”.Pub. L. 91–375

Effective Date of 1970 AmendmentAmendment by effective within 1 year after , on date established therefor by Board of Governors of United States Postal Service and published by it in Federal Register, see , set out as an Effective Date note preceding , Postal Service.Pub.
